sections.value: RE: PETITION ON A CASE OF CULPABLE HOMICIDE INVOLVING LATE MR MICHAEL CHI
A petition dated 2nd march, 2026, was lodged with the ministry of justice by Mr maechi matt. the petition details serious allegations of murder.

value:
sometime in April, 2025, Mr Micheal chi was brutally murdered. he was shot multiple times, while he was asleep in his room. an eyewitness claimed that the accused broke into the deceased's room at about 11:00 am, while he was sleeping.

However, the matter remains unsolved, although the petitioner claims that other persons might also be involved.

The petitioner further revealed, that on 13th april, 2025, the mr michael was found dead in his room, with multiple identifiable bullet holes in his body.

on 12th october, 2025 the suspect was later apprehended in choba town, in port-harcourt, by a joint investigation team led by ASP joseph bello

according to the petitioner, the suspect was later released under circumstances that remain unclear (according to the investigative officers, the suspect was released on bail), with no notice was provided to the petitioner/complainant.

a. an order for urgent administrative and judicial review of the culpable homicide case involving mr michael chi.

b. to investigate the alleged abuse of bail procedure.

c. to direct that the accused be properly charged and brought before a competent court of law.

d. to grant any further relief necessary to prevent a miscarriage of justice.
a. the legal and procedural validity of the investigation into the alleged culpable homicide.
b. the procedural irregularities, as it relates to bail and how it may obstruct the administration of justice.
a. the 1999 constitution of the federal republic of nigeria.
- particularly fair hearing. see section 36 of the 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

a. Culpable homicide punishable with death is a grave offence under Nigerian criminal law, and the grant of bail in such matters is generally exercised cautiously by the courts. Where administrative bail is granted under questionable circumstances without proper documentation or procedural safeguards, such action may warrant administrative review.

b. Although the suspect is entitled to the presumption of innocence and fair hearing under Section 36 of the Constitution, the complainant and the society at large are also entitled to a fair and transparent criminal justice process. Any arbitrary release of a suspect without due process may compromise the administration of justice.

c. By virtue of Section 174 of the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria 1999 (as amended), the Attorney-General possesses wide prosecutorial powers, including the power to institute, take over, continue, or discontinue criminal proceedings. Consequently, the Attorney-General may direct a review of the investigation and prosecution of the matter where necessary in the interest of justice.

d. Considering the apparent inconsistencies surrounding the arrest and subsequent release of the suspect, there is sufficient basis for an independent administrative review to determine whether due process was followed by the investigating authorities.

e. The allegation that other persons may have participated in the commission of the offence raises the need for further investigation to determine whether there was conspiracy, aiding, or common intention in the alleged crime.

sections.value: It is recommended that the case diary, investigation report, and bail documentation relating to the matter be reviewed by the Ministry of Justice to determine whether the prosecution can be sustained based on available evidence.

value:
J. J. esq
chief state counsel
Homicide unit
2nd may, 2026
